text: You mustn't enter the ICU alone.
You have to sign in at reception.
You shouldn't run in the corridor.

Hi Graziele, this is Doctor Carter. A few rules for our guest tomorrow. He must wear a badge and a mask. He has to sign in at reception. He should wash his hands, and he shouldn't enter the ICU alone. Please share these rules with him. Kind and clear. You will do great.

Hi Graziele, it is Ana. A quick tip about rules. Use must for a strong rule: you must wear a badge. Use mustn't for something forbidden: you mustn't enter the ICU alone. Use should for advice: you should wash your hands. Keep it simple. You will do great.

Graziele explains the hospital rules to a visitor. First, the badge: you must wear a badge. Then, the sign-in: you have to sign in at reception. Next, the mask: you must wear a mask in the ICU. After that, the hands: you should wash your hands. Finally, the corridor: you shouldn't run in the corridor.
You mustn't enter the ICU alone.
You must wear a mask in the ICU.
You have to sign in at reception.
You shouldn't run in the corridor.
